Limited Liability Company (LLC): Common structure, considered a separate legal entity from its shareholders
Sole Proprietorship: Business owned and operated by a single individual, with no legal distinction between the business and the owner
Partnership: Businesses jointly operated by two or more people.
Branches of Foreign Companies: Foreign companies can operate as branches if registered with the Investment Promotion Authority (IPA).
Associations/Non-Profit Organizations: For charitable, religious, or community-focused operations.
